evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern contains inline step comments: '// Step 1: Get predicates/collocates for the seed noun using the BCQL pattern', '// Step 2: For each collocate, find nouns it collocates with', etc. These restate what the method calls make obvious — restating comments without added insight.

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern has debug logging on entry (logger.debug with all parameters) and exit (logger.debug 'Exploration complete'). This is entry/exit logging but the entry log is not excessive — it names the specific parameter values. Borderline.

RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias: `if (relation == null) throw new IllegalArgumentException("relation must not be null");` — the method has no @NonNull annotation, so the null check is appropriate. Not an AI debt pattern.

SemanticFieldExplorer: `@NonNull String seed` parameter in exploreByPattern (public overload) has no Objects.requireNonNull; the package-private overload checks `if (seed.isEmpty())` but not null — the @NonNull annotation is unenforced at runtime. Defensive null checks are absent where @NonNull is declared.
Main.handleBlackLabServerCommand: extensive println output to stdout for server startup (Index, Port, Loaded grammar config) — this is user-facing CLI output, not debug logging, so it is appropriate.
GrammarConfigLoader.parse: `logger.info("Loaded grammar config version {}: {} relations{}", ...)` — a single info log at load time. Appropriate.

HttpApiUtils.wrapWithErrorHandling has three catch clauses (IAE → warn, IOException → error, Exception → error). Three separate catch blocks for three distinct severity levels is design, not boilerplate.

CollocateProfileComparator.buildAdjectiveProfileList has a comment: '// Second pass for variance (requires the mean — unavoidable two-pass for numerical stability)' — explains WHY, not WHAT. Good comment.

Several Javadoc comments restate the method name: CollocateProfileComparator.compareCollocateProfiles first paragraph restates 'Compares adjective collocate profiles across a set of seed nouns' which matches the class and method name exactly. Similarly, MultiSeedExplorer.computeCollocateIntersection Javadoc first line restates 'Fetches collocates for each seed... then intersects them'.
ExplorationResult field comment: `private final Map<String, Long> seedCollocateFrequencies;  // collocate -> raw frequency` — this comment adds nothing to the field name seedCollocateFrequencies.

SemanticFieldExplorer has 60+ lines of class-level Javadoc including HTML lists, example usage, and design notes. The design notes are genuinely informative (e.g. 'Design: coordination facade for ExplorationHandlers') but the example usage section ('Seed: house / Top adjectives: beautiful, old, big...') reads as explanatory scaffolding that may have been generated to explain the algorithm.

BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeCollocations has a null/empty guard on lemma with a debug log: 'executeCollocations: skipping query — lemma is null or empty'. The method is not annotated @NonNull for lemma, so the guard is appropriate (documented in QueryExecutor interface).
No identical boilerplate error handling copied verbatim across multiple files — wrapWithErrorHandling centralises this.
No pass-through wrapper functions with no added logic other than the already-noted wrapHandler case.
LogDiceCalculator.compute(long, long, long) overload: `return compute((double) collocateFreq, ...)` — a legitimate convenience overload, not a boilerplate pass-through.

RelationConfig has an inline comment on the collocatePosGroup field: `/** Pre-computed collocate POS group — cached at construction time so repeated calls are O(1). */` — explains WHY the field exists, a good comment.

strengths: No identical boilerplate error handling copied across files — the uniform error boundary is centralised in wrapWithErrorHandling.
No generic names (handleData, processItem) in domain code — domain terms are used consistently.
No noisy debug logging of full object dumps — debug calls are scoped to specific parameter values.
Key architectural decisions are explained with genuine rationale comments (e.g., why variance requires two passes, why wrapHandler exists, why ExplorationResult is a class not a record).
ai_generated_debt.issue_character: The primary AI-debt pattern is restating comments (step comments in exploreByPattern, Javadoc first lines that repeat method names) and a verbose class-level example in SemanticFieldExplorer that functions as explanatory scaffolding rather than architectural documentation.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: The codebase is mostly clean of AI-hallmark patterns: no boilerplate copying, no generic domain names, no full-object debug dumps, no defensive null checks on @NonNull parameters. The score stops at 72 because of a consistent pattern of step comments that restate code and Javadoc first sentences that mirror the method/class name, which are classic indicators of over-documented AI-assisted code generation.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern uses inline step comments that restate what the method calls already communicate
evidence: Comment `// Step 1: Get predicates/collocates for the seed noun using the BCQL pattern` above `List<QueryResults.WordSketchResult> seedRelations = fetchSeedCollocates(...)` — the method name fetchSeedCollocates already says this.
Comment `// Step 2: For each collocate, find nouns it collocates with` above `buildCollocateToNounsMap(...)` — the method name says this.

Comment `// Step 3: Score nouns by shared collocate count` above `filterByMinShared(...)` — the method name is slightly misnamed (filterByMinShared does scoring and filtering), but the comment restates rather than clarifies.
Comment `// Step 4: Identify core collocates` above `identifyCoreCollocates(...)` — pure restatement.
Comments `// Build map: collocate -> logDice with seed` and `// Build map: collocate -> logDice with seed` above map-building loops that are self-evident from variable names.
issues.suggestion: Remove the step comments that restate method names. If the step numbering is valuable for explaining the algorithm flow, move the numbered explanation to the class-level Javadoc rather than inline in the method body. The phase-method names are expressive enough to guide readers.

issues.summary: Several Javadoc first sentences mirror the method or class name without adding information
evidence: CollocateProfileComparator.compareCollocateProfiles first sentence: 'Compare adjective collocate profiles across a set of seed nouns...' — the method name already says compareCollocateProfiles.
MultiSeedExplorer.computeCollocateIntersection first sentence: 'Fetches collocates for each seed using the given relation, then intersects them across seeds...' — mostly restates the method name.
ExplorationResult field comment: `// collocate -> raw frequency` next to the field named seedCollocateFrequencies — the field name already encodes this.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite Javadoc first sentences to add information not in the method name: document the key precondition, the interpretation of the return value, or a non-obvious side effect. Remove field comments that duplicate the field name. Good Javadoc adds something the reader cannot derive from the name alone.

root_causes: Step comments and name-restating Javadoc are consistent with AI-assisted code generation where documentation is added in a pass after the code is written, without reviewing whether comments add value beyond what method names already say.

The verbose SemanticFieldExplorer class Javadoc example (house → beautiful, old, big) reads as explanatory scaffolding that would be valuable during development but should be trimmed to architectural intent for production code.
possible_false_positives: The SemanticFieldExplorer class-level example — it may be genuinely useful documentation for contributors new to NLP collocate analysis, not just AI scaffolding
